events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need assess hazard: shackle side-loaded. In rigging, shackle should not be side-loaded; ASME B30.26 and OSHA 1926.251 require in-line loading, side loading reduces capacity and can cause failure. Decision fail because dangerous/non-compliant. Risk high/critical? Side loading of shackle under lift is critical potential dropped load. Let's set risk critical. Work_stage in-progress. Component conditions installed-defective and non-compliant. Percent complete maybe 50? Value 0. Sound etc not applicable default empty string 0.0 etc. Findings: shackle is side-loaded causing reduction in WLL and potential pin/body failure; non-compliant with ASME B30 and OSHA. Actions: cease lift/operation; remove/reposition shackle to align load with centerline; replace if deformed; re-inspect rigging by competent person; verify rated capacity and tag lines; conduct pre-lift meeting. References ASME B30.26 (Rigging Hardware) and OSHA 1926.251. Need output raw JSON no fences.

events.label: response
events.text:
{
  "decision": "fail",
  "risk": "critical",
  "work_stage": "in-progress",
  "component_conditions": ["installed-defective", "non-compliant"],
  "percent_complete": 50,
  "value": 0,
  "sound_source": "",
  "confidence": 0.95,
  "event_time": 0.0,

  "order": [],

  "findings": [
    "Shackle is side-loaded, reducing its working load limit and creating risk of pin or body failure.",
    "Side loading violates rigging hardware requirements and creates a dropped-load hazard.",
    "Configuration is non-compliant with applicable rigging standards."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Immediately stop the lift and remove personnel from the drop zone.",
    "Remove the side-loaded shackle and reconfigure rigging so the load is applied in line with the shackle centerline.",
    "Inspect the shackle and associated rigging for deformation, wear, or damage; replace if any deficiency is found.",
    "Have a qualified/competent person re-inspect the rigging configuration before resuming operations.",
    "Verify rated capacity, sling angles, and rigging plan compliance before next lift."
  ],
  "rationale": "Side-loaded shackles lose rated capacity and can fail suddenly under load, presenting an imminent struck-by/dropped-load hazard; ASME B30 and OSHA 1926.251 require rigging hardware to be loaded in the intended plane.",
  "references": [
    "ASME B30.26 - Rigging Hardware",
    "OSHA 1926.251 - Rigging equipment for material handling"
  ]
}
